libraryDescription:
This library, gui.ButtonPanel, provides a panel containing arranged buttons as a GUI component.

In the standard VCSSL library GUI, GUI components are handled as int IDs, but the GUI component provided by this module is handled as theButtonPanel struct.

The ButtonPanel struct internally stores resource IDs and related data, but you normally do not need to be aware of its internals becausefunctions for creating and destroying it are provided. Direct access to its members is not recommended.
Specifically, create it with the newButtonPanel function and destroy it with thedeleteComponent function.

This module also provides overloads of standard GUI library functions so that the ButtonPanel struct can be handled almost in the sameway as in the standard library GUI.
For example, the mountComponent, setComponentSize, andsetComponentLocation functions are provided.

function0.name: newButtonPanel
function0.declaration: ButtonPanel newButtonPanel(int x, int y, int width, int height, string labelText[ ])
function0.parameters:
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the panel. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the panel. 
(int type) width : The width of the panel. 
(int type) height : The height of the panel. 
(string[] type) labelText : The array storing the label text. 
buttonWidth : The width of the button. 
buttonText : The array storing the text of the buttons.
function0.returnValue: (ButtonPanel type) The created button panel struct.

function1.name: deleteComponent
function1.parameters: (ButtonPanel type) panelInstance : The button panel to be destroyed.

function2.name: paintComponent
function2.parameters: (ButtonPanel type) panelInstance : The button panel to be repainted.

function3.name: mountComponent
function3.description: Mounts a button panel onto another GUI component.
function3.parameters:
(ButtonPanel type) panelInstance : The button panel to be mounted. 
(int type) componentID : The destination GUI component ID.

function4.name: demountComponent
function4.description: Demounts a button panel from another GUI component.
function4.parameters:
(ButtonPanel type) panelInstance : The button panel to be demounted. 
(int type) componentID : The destination GUI component ID.

function5.name: getPanel
function5.description: Returns the component ID of the panel.
function5.parameters: (ButtonPanel type) panelInstance : The target button panel.
function5.returnValue: (int type) The GUI component ID of the panel.

function6.name: getButton
function6.description: Gets the button corresponding to the specified label.
function6.parameters:
(ButtonPanel type) panelInstance : The target button panel. 
(string type) labelText : The label text corresponding to the button to get.
function6.returnValue: (int type) The GUI component ID of the button.

function7.name: getButton
function7.description: Gets the button at the specified index.
function7.parameters:
(ButtonPanel type) panelInstance : The target button panel. 
(int type) index : The index of the button.
function7.returnValue: (int type) The GUI component ID of the button.

function8.name: getComponentSize
function8.description: Returns the size of the panel.
function8.parameters: (ButtonPanel type) panelInstance : The target button panel.
function8.returnValue: (int[] type) The array storing the size of the panel.

function9.name: setComponentSize
function9.declaration: void setComponentSize(ButtonPanel &panelInstance, int width, int height)
function9.description: Sets the size of the panel.
function9.parameters:
(ButtonPanel type) panelInstance : The target button panel. 
(int type) width : The width of the panel. 
(int type) height : The height of the panel.

function10.name: setComponentSize
function10.description: Automatically adjusts the sizes of the internal components to match the current panel size. Use this when the panel size has been changed externally.
function10.parameters: (ButtonPanel type) panelInstance : The target button panel.

function11.name: getComponentLocation
function11.description: Returns the location of the panel.
function11.parameters: (ButtonPanel type) panelInstance : The target button panel.
function11.returnValue: (int[] type) The array storing the location of the panel.

function12.name: setComponentLocation
function12.declaration: void setComponentLocation(ButtonPanel &panelInstance, int x, int y)
function12.description: Sets the location of the panel.
function12.parameters:
(ButtonPanel type) panelInstance : The target button panel. 
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the window. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the window.
